questions: Were all four implementation steps completed as planned?
Does the implementation match all ticket acceptance criteria?
Are there any deviations from the implementation plan?

answers.answer: Yes. All four steps were completed: (1) perform.ts refactored from execSync to spawnSync with stderr capture, (2) validate.ts created with validateInstall(), (3) validation inserted into runUpdate() before saveConfig, (4) validation inserted into checkAutoUpdate() before saveConfig.
evidence: src/update/perform.ts: uses spawnSync with shell:true, encoding:utf8, returns {success, error?, stderr?}
src/update/validate.ts: new file with validateInstall() using npm root -g, existsSync, and node --version check
src/update/index.ts line 80-93: validation gate in runUpdate with error, stderr, and recovery messaging
src/update/index.ts line 155-162: validation gate in checkAutoUpdate with warn-and-continue behavior

answers.answer: Yes. (1) Valid installs continue to work with success message. (2) Missing dist/index.js causes exit(1) with the missing path. (3) npm stderr is surfaced on validation failure. (4) Declaration-only dist/ is not accepted. (5) Recovery message mentions rebuild/relink. (6) Existing behavior unchanged for valid installs.
evidence: CHK-05 output: {valid:true} for intact global install
CHK-04 output: {valid:false, error:'Bin target missing: ...'} for missing index.js
index.ts line 84-85: result.stderr surfaced when available
index.ts line 87-91: recovery hint printed on failure

answers.answer: One minor deviation: spawnSync calls use full command strings instead of separate command+args arrays to avoid Node.js DEP0190 deprecation warnings. This is functionally equivalent and more forward-compatible.
evidence: Node 22+ emits DEP0190 when args array is passed with shell:true
perform.ts line 17: spawnSync(`npm install -g ${installSpec}`, ...) instead of spawnSync('npm', ['install', '-g', installSpec], ...)
validate.ts line 20, 59: same pattern for npm root -g and node --version calls

answers.answer: No unresolved followups. All changes are confined to src/update/ as planned. All verification checks pass.
evidence: CHK-04: validates missing bin target correctly

statement: Implementation complete. Three files changed in src/update/: perform.ts refactored from execSync to spawnSync with stderr capture, new validate.ts with validateInstall() for post-install integrity checks, and index.ts modified to gate metadata save and success messaging on validation in both manual and auto-update paths. All five verification checks pass. One minor deviation: spawnSync uses command strings instead of separate args to avoid DEP0190 deprecation.
